programs.name: Senior CommUnity Care of Kentucky (PACE)
programs.what_it_does: Provides comprehensive health care including primary and specialty medical care, prescription medications, adult day services, home care, mental health services, therapies, and nursing home or hospital care to seniors who need nursing facility level of care but wish to live at home.[1][3][4] An interdisciplinary team coordinates all health and social needs to help participants maintain independence in the community.[1][3]
programs.who_its_for: Individuals age 55 and older in specific Kentucky counties (e.g., Jefferson, Boone, Campbell, Kenton, Gallatin, Carroll, Owen, Grant, Pendleton) who require nursing facility level of care but can live safely in the community with support.[1][3][4]
programs.whats_unique: Kentucky-specific PACE providers operating in designated counties, state-supported to expand coverage for home-based care beyond standard federal Medicare/Medicaid PACE availability nationwide (over 125 providers exist nationally).[1][3][4]
programs.benefit_value: All-inclusive care covering full spectrum of medical and supportive services; over 90% of participants with high care needs remain in community.[1][4]

programs.name: Senior Companion Program (Community Action of Southern Kentucky)
programs.what_it_does: Provides assistance to older adults with health issues or loneliness through regular friendly visits, helping with daily living tasks to retain dignity and independence.[2] Offers socialization, decreased loneliness, improved home functioning, and respite for family caregivers.[2]
programs.who_its_for: Older adults in Allen, Barren, Butler, Edmonson, Hart, Logan, Metcalfe, Monroe, Simpson, and Warren counties who have health issues, loneliness, or difficulty with daily tasks; volunteers are 55+ providing at least 15 hours/week.[2]
programs.whats_unique: Regionally operated state volunteer program in southern Kentucky counties using senior volunteers (with stipends) for companionship and light assistance, not a universal federal program but locally implemented via community action agencies.[2]
programs.benefit_value: Non-taxable hourly stipend for volunteers (does not affect benefits), paid holidays, training; for recipients: increased socialization, functioning, and caregiver respite.[2]

programs.name: Kentucky Family Caregiver Program (Grandparents Raising Grandchildren)
programs.what_it_does: Offers state-funded supportive services including information on resources, counseling, support groups, caregiver training, and financial assistance for clothing, respite, educational supplies, legal services, and other expenses.[6] Helps grandparents access services to manage caregiving responsibilities.[6]
programs.who_its_for: Grandparents of any age in Kentucky who are primary caregivers for grandchildren under 18, meet financial guidelines, and are not receiving Kinship Care.[6]
programs.whats_unique: State-funded program specifically supporting grandparents raising grandchildren with financial and service assistance, distinct from standard federal senior benefits and targeted at intergenerational caregiving in Kentucky.[6]
programs.benefit_value: Financial assistance for authorized expenses like clothing, respite, education, and legal aid; plus counseling, training, and support group access.[6]
